title: Single-Pass Classifier-Free Guidance for Diffusion Transformers via Conditioning Interpolation
description: We propose Conditioning-Space Guidance (CSG), a training-free method that approximates classifier-free guidance (CFG) in a single forward pass by extrapolating in the AdaLN parameter space of Diffusion Transformers. Instead of running two full forward passes (conditional + unconditional) and combining their outputs, CSG computes the AdaLN scale/shift/gate parameters for both conditions using the cheap conditioning MLPs, linearly extrapolates them, and runs a single transformer forward pass with the guided parameters. This achieves approximately 2x inference speedup while maintaining quality comparable to standard CFG.
motivation: Classifier-Free Guidance doubles the computational cost of inference in diffusion/flow matching models by requiring two full forward passes per denoising step. This is especially burdensome for large Diffusion Transformer (DiT) models. While methods like guidance distillation (AGD) reduce this cost, they require additional training. Feature caching methods (TaylorSeer, AB-Cache) still require two passes when they compute. No existing training-free method achieves single-pass CFG by exploiting the specific AdaLN conditioning structure of DiT architectures.
proposed_approach: CSG operates in three steps: (1) Compute AdaLN parameters (scale, shift, gate) for both conditional and unconditional inputs using the lightweight AdaLN MLPs (~0.1% of total compute); (2) Linearly extrapolate these parameters using the CFG formula: params_guided = params_uncond + w * (params_cond - params_uncond); (3) Run a single transformer forward pass with the guided AdaLN parameters. We also propose per-layer guidance (CSG-PL) with layer-specific guidance weights, and a hybrid approach (CSG-H) that uses full CFG at critical timesteps. The method exploits the key observation that AdaLN is linear in its scale/shift parameters, so interpolation in AdaLN parameter space approximates interpolation in output space.

related_work.title: Classifier-Free Diffusion Guidance
related_work.relation: The foundational guidance method that our work accelerates. CFG combines conditional and unconditional predictions via linear interpolation of model outputs. CSG achieves the same linear interpolation but in conditioning parameter space, reducing the two forward passes to one.

related_work.title: Scalable Diffusion Models with Transformers
related_work.relation: Introduces the DiT architecture with AdaLN conditioning. CSG specifically exploits the AdaLN structure of DiT for efficient guidance.

related_work.title: SiT: Exploring Flow and Diffusion-based Generative Models with Scalable Interpolant Transformers
related_work.authors: Nanye Ma, Mark Goldstein, Michael S. Albergo, Nicholas M. Boffi, Eric Vanden-Eijnden, Saining Xie
related_work.relation: Extends DiT to flow matching with interpolant framework, achieving state-of-the-art FID. Uses the same AdaLN architecture, making it a natural target for CSG.

related_work.title: Flow Matching for Generative Modeling
related_work.authors: Yaron Lipman, Ricky T.Q. Chen, Heli Ben-Hamu, Maximilian Nickel, Matt Le
related_work.relation: Foundational work on flow matching that underpins SiT. CSG is applicable to both diffusion and flow matching models.

related_work.title: Eliminating Oversaturation and Artifacts of High Guidance Scales in Diffusion Models
related_work.authors: Seyedmorteza Sadat, Otmar Hilliges, Romann M. Weber
related_work.relation: APG decomposes CFG into parallel/perpendicular components to fix oversaturation. CSG addresses a different problem (efficiency, not direction). They can be combined: apply APG's projection to CSG's single-pass output.

related_work.title: Efficient Distillation of Classifier-Free Guidance using Adapters
related_work.relation: AGD trains adapters to approximate CFG in a single pass. Unlike AGD, CSG is completely training-free and requires no additional parameters. AGD requires access to training data and GPU time for distillation.

related_work.title: CFG-Zero*: Improved Classifier-Free Guidance for Flow Matching Models
related_work.authors: Weichen Fan, Amber Yijia Zheng, Raymond A. Yeh, Ziwei Liu
related_work.relation: Fixes guidance issues specific to flow matching (inaccurate early-step velocity). CSG is orthogonal - it addresses computational cost, not guidance quality. The zero-init technique from CFG-Zero* can be combined with CSG.

related_work.title: Applying Guidance in a Limited Interval Improves Sample and Distribution Quality in Diffusion Models
related_work.authors: Tuomas Kynkaanniemi, Miika Aittala, Samuli Laine, Jaakko Lehtinen, Tero Karras
related_work.relation: Shows guidance is only beneficial in a middle interval of timesteps. CSG is compatible with guidance interval - use CSG within the active interval for compounded speedup.

hypothesis: The output of DiT/SiT models is approximately linear in the AdaLN conditioning parameters (scale, shift, gate), enabling single-pass CFG via conditioning-space extrapolation that achieves image quality (FID, IS) comparable to standard two-pass CFG with approximately 2x inference speedup.

confirms: CSG achieves FID within 10% of standard CFG on ImageNet 256x256 at guidance scale w=4.0
CSG achieves at least 1.7x throughput improvement over standard CFG
CSG-H (hybrid with 20% full CFG steps) matches standard CFG quality within 5% FID
Per-layer guidance (CSG-PL) discovers meaningful layer-dependent guidance patterns
refutes: CSG FID is more than 25% worse than standard CFG even at low guidance scales (w < 3)
The conditioning linearity assumption breaks down catastrophically (approximation error > 50%)
No per-layer guidance schedule improves upon uniform guidance
